source.attribution: Official source pages and configured public event feeds

events.title: A Bad Case of Stripes: The Musical
events.description: In this fun musical adaptation of David Shannon’s beloved book, Camilla thinks she must choose between her favorite food – lima beans – and the acceptance of those around her. But the harder she tries to conform to other people’s opinions, the more miserable (and colorful) she becomes. With infectious energy and a heartfelt message, A Bad Case of Stripes rem
events.venue: San Diego Junior Theatre at Casa del Prado Theatre

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks , and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Pale
events.venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

events.title: Tai Chi Ch’uan Lee Style
events.description: A class session consists of gentle limbering exercises that aid in coordination, balance, and the opening of various channels within the body to allow your Chi to flow freely. In addition, we incorporate respiration therapy such as our style’s form of Qigong, K’ai Men, otherwise known as Taoist Yoga and various specialized breathing exercises referred to as

events.title: Art Mart
events.description: SDCAMA art shows display the living art of today; you can hold it in your hands and take it into your life. Many of our artist members are full time professionals that have been plying their trade for decades, have won competitions throughout the U.S., and are world renowned. Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy
events.venue: Park Boulevard and Presidents Way Lawn

events.title: Clearly Indigenous: Native Visions Reimagined in Glass
events.description: Explore how Indigenous artists fuse heritage and contemporary glassmaking to illuminate their stories, connection to nature, and cultural traditions. events.title: Pride Party
events.description: Enjoy a family-friendly Pride celebration at the Museum of Us! Registered guests enjoy free admission, activities with community partners, and special guests – Queens from The Haus Of St. James. events.title: Tomo Charms
events.description: Craft your own one-of-a-kind beaded keychain in this hands-on knotting workshop! Using paracord colors inspired by the Garden and an eclectic mix of beads, including some sourced directly from Japan, you’ll assemble a custom keychain! *Admission and all supplies included in pricing. The workshop begins promptly at 11am in the Education Center. Parking is not

events.title: Natsu Nights
events.description: A Summer Night Market Series Set inside the Japanese Friendship Garden & Museum, this night market series transforms the garden into a different atmosphere. Explore layered landscapes as you move through the space after dark, and let the garden become a welcoming place where you can gather, linger, and spend time outside the usual routine. events.title: Mixed Media Art Class
events.description: Join a Japanese artist to create a beautiful mixed media art of gold fish. The guest will make the 9×12” size that would be perfect to display at their home for the Spring season. events.title: Astronmica
events.description: It’s in our nature to look up at the night sky and wonder: Where did we come from? From the visionary creator of Mesmerica and Beautifica comes Astronomica , an all-new immersive dome experience. Drift through auroras, dance through the stars, and discover worlds beyond your wildest dreams in this breathtaking journey through the infinite beauty of the cosmo

events.title: A Journey Through Japanese Tea & Chakabuki
events.description: Join us for an experience with Tokyo-based tea expert Takahiro Okabe—tea producer, curator, educator, and founder of Japan’s first Tea Brewing Championship. This immersive program invites participants to explore the rich diversity and evolving creativity of Japanese tea culture. Moving beyond familiar green teas, guests will discover a deeply roasted hojicha

events.title: Rakugo
events.description: Join us for an engaging introduction to rakugo , Japan’s traditional form of comedic storytelling, performed by Kyoto-based tea master and cultural practitioner Takahiro Okabe. events.description: All levels, all ages class taught by Eduardo Santana every Monday. There is a fee of $20 per class. If you would like to attend a class, wear shoes that allow for pivoting.

events.title: Tanabata Festival
events.description: Originating from Chinese folklore, Tanabata is a holiday that celebrates the meeting of Orihime and Hikoboshi! Separated by the Milky Way, the stars Orihime and Hikoboshi are able to meet once per year on the seventh day of the seventh month. Guests of all ages are invited to enjoy the summer sun and partake in the Tanabata celebration. This year’s festival

events.title: Community Art Workshop
events.description: Great for artists of all ages. Community Art Workshops invite artists to enjoy hands-on art making experiences inspired by the thinking and creating processes of artists on view at the Museum. Artists under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of the workshop. Please join us! No reservation is required. All materials are provided. F

events.title: Home Away From Home
events.description: Join us for a screening of the documentary Home Away from Home , which provides an intimate look at the work of South Korean artist Do Ho Suh. Renowned for his “suitcase architecture,” Suh uses hand-stitched silk and translucent fabrics to create 1:1 scale replicas of the homes he has lived in across the globe. By recreating these intimate spaces, he explore

events.title: Knot Together
events.description: Inspired by the traditional fishing nets and nautical knots featured in To Catch a Fish , our newest Together workshop series, Knot Together , explores the art of macramé. events.title: Cecil Beaton’s Fashionable World
events.description: Photographer, fashion illustrator, Oscar-winning costume designer, social caricaturist, and writer Cecil Beaton (1904–1980) was an extraordinary force in the twentieth-century British and American creative scenes. This is the first exhibition dedicated to Beaton’s fashion and portrait photography, which he elevated into an art form. This exhibition showcases

events.title: Art Break
events.description: Join us for a day of community and craft as we celebrate the opening of Clearly Indigenous . Throughout the day, we invite you to explore the vibrant continuity of Indigenous artistry, where time-honored traditions meet modern creative expression. We invite you to a special Art Break with exhibiting glass artist Dan Friday. Dan Friday is a member of the Lumm

events.title: Celebrating Clearly Indigenous
events.description: Join us for a day of community and craft as we celebrate the opening of Clearly Indigenous: Native Visions Reimagined in Glass . Enjoy free admission all day as we invite you to explore the exhibition and experience the vibrant continuity of Indigenous artistry, where time-honored traditions meet modern creative expression.

events.title: Umeboshi Making Class
events.description: Austin Durant from Fermenters Club will teach you how to make Umeboshi from scratch. Umeboshi are traditional Japanese pickled, salt-cured plums. This class will be in two parts. events.title: Brunch at The Nat
events.description: Turn your museum visit into Sunday Funday with the addition of brunch at The Nat. Plan a Sunday visit that includes the full Nat experience, from bones to barnacles to brunch. events.title: House of France Lawn Program
events.description: The annual lawn Program for the House of France, a.k.a. the French cottage or le cottage français, takes places in July to celebrate Bastille Day or le 14 juillet as the French call it. events.title: Film Screening: Love Birds
events.description: Celebrate Pride Week the right way—with “gay gulls.” Love Birds follows the incredible story of two scientists as they discover lesbian seagulls living on California’s Channel Islands, and how their work helped shape LGBTQ+ history. events.title: Cyrano
events.description: Brilliant poet, dazzling swordsman, and hopeless romantic, Cyrano is as quick with a quip as he is with a blade. But behind his bluster lies shame about his physical appearance. events.title: Pride 5K Run + Walk
events.description: The Pride 5K Run & Walk is always a highlight of Pride Week festivities. As many as 1,700 runners and walkers, from around the world, participate and raise approximately $40,000 for our charity partners. events.title: Fleet Accessibility Mornings
events.description: Early access to galleries at 9 a.m. On the third Saturday of every month, we open our doors one hour before the museum opens to the public and screen a documentary with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. events.title: Globe Pride AXIS Event
events.description: Visitors are welcomed onto our campus to attend our fun and festive AXIS events which are designed for the whole family. events.title: Coffee and Conversations
events.description: In an age of digital immediacy, what continues to draw us to books?
